data.title: Box ticks up as BofA starts with Buy rating
data.description:
• Box (NYSE:BOX) was in focus on Thursday as Bank of America initiated coverage on the cloud storage company with a Buy rating and $40 price target.
  • Shares rose 0.8% in premarket trading.
  • "As the leading enterprise data repository, we think [Box] is well positioned for AI monetization opportunities as the market transitions from simply storing data and documents to extracting value, insights and increased productivity by leveraging AI," analyst Michael Funk wrote in a note to clients. "Moreover, enterprise headwinds (like tighter budgets and right-sized headcount) are fading yet valuation at 13.4x Street C25E FCF still reflects an overly bearish view of revenue growth and margin expansion potential, in our view, given a raft of new product offerings (Box AI Studio, Workflow, Security and Box Doc Gen). Finally, our DCF analysis points to a compelling risk-reward profile, in our view."
  • Delving a bit deeper, Funk said he sees Box's Intelligent Content Management portfolio expanding its total addressable market to $26B by 2028. And with the market as "fragmented" as it is, Box should be able to "carve out a substantial niche based on its entrenched position as an enterprise data repository and compelling AI product roadmap," he added.

data.title: Sendero Resources looks to raise C$1.68M via private placement
data.description:
• Sendero Resources (TSXV:SEND:CA) said it will conduct a non-brokered private placement of up to 14M units at C$0.12 apiece to raise up to C$1.68M.
  • The company said each unit will have one share and one share purchase warrant being exercisable to purchase one share at C$0.16 apiece.
  • The firm expects to use the proceeds to develop its mineral property interests in Argentina, for general administrative expenses and working capital purposes.
  • The company added certain directors and officers of of the firm may acquire securities under the placement.

data.title: Nvidia's request to avoid securities fraud suit dismissed by US Supreme Court - report
data.description:
Update: The story was updated with Nvidia's statement.

On Wednesday, The U.S. Supreme Court rejected an appeal by Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) looking to avoid a securities fraud lawsuit by stockholders, who alleged that the company misled investors about how much of its sales relied on the cryptocurrency market, Reuters reported.

The judges, who heard arguments in the case on Nov. 13, decided not to give a decision on the underlying legal dispute and instead dismissed Nvidia's appeal of a lower court's decision which allowed the 2018 class action to go ahead, the report added.

The Supreme Court, in determining that it should not have granted the appeal, kept the lower court's ruling in place, the report noted.

The lawsuit is led by Sweden-based investment management firm E. Ohman J:or Fonder AB.

The Supreme Court's gave no explanation for the dismissal, according to the report.

During the arguments, some of the justices showed reservations about intervening in the case, saying that if there was a clear legal issue for them to decide, as opposed to only a dispute over facts. They also indicated that they were not ideally placed to resolve the lawsuit due to the technical complexities, the report added.

At issue was if the plaintiffs cleared the legal threshold for bringing private securities fraud suits set under a 1995 federal law known as the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act which aimed to filter out frivolous lawsuit.

The plaintiffs alleged that Nvidia and the company's CEO Jensen Huang violated a 1934 federal law known as the Securities Exchange Act by making statements in 2017 and 2018 which falsely downplayed how much of Nvidia's revenue increase came from crypto-related purchases, the report noted.

Starting in 2017, as the price of some cryptocurrencies surged, Nvidia's chips became popular for cryptomining. However, by late 2018 amidst a decline in crypto, Nvidia's revenue did not meet its forecast, leading its stock price to fall in early November that year, the report added.

The lawsuit has requested unspecified monetary damages, partly to regain the lost value of the Nvidia stock held by the investors, as per the report.

In 2022, Nvidia agreed to pay $5.5M to U.S. authorities to resolve charges that it did not clearly disclose the impact of cryptomining on its gaming business, however, without admitting or denying the findings of the regulators, the report noted.

A federal judge had dismissed the stockholder suit, however, the San Francisco-based 9th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals revived it. The Appeals court found that the plaintiffs had adequately alleged that Huang made "false or misleading statements and did so knowingly or recklessly," permitting the case to go ahead, the report added.

"We would have preferred a decision on the merits affirming the trial court’s dismissal of the case, but we are fully prepared to continue our defense. Consistent and predictable standards in securities litigation are essential to protecting shareholders and ensuring a strong economy, and we remain committed to supporting them," said an Nvidia spokesperson in an emailed statement to Seeking Alpha.

Deepak Gupta, who represented the stockholders before the Supreme Court, said the dismissal was "a win for corporate accountability," as per the report.

Nvidia said to the Supreme Court that the plaintiffs failed to properly show that the disputed corporate statements were false or that Nvidia had intentionally or recklessly misled investors, as needed by the law, the report added.

In return, the plaintiffs said that the lawsuit has adequate allegations — obtained from former employees, market analysis and expert opinion — to survive the company's request to dismiss the suit, which now moves to the discovery stage of the litigation, the report noted.

The Biden administration had supported the stockholders in the case.

The Nvidia lawsuit was one of the two cases which came before the Supreme Court in November that involved the right of private litigants to hold companies accountable for alleged securities fraud. The other case, which was related to Meta Platforms (META), was argued on Nov. 6 and similarly dismissed by the justices on Nov. 22, the report added.

data.title: Virtus Investment declares $2.25 dividend
data.description:

data.title: The Lovesac reports mixed Q3 results; initiates Q4 and updates FY outlook
data.description:
• The Lovesac press release (NASDAQ:LOVE): Q3 GAAP EPS of -$0.32 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $149.91M (-2.7% Y/Y) misses by $5.35M.
  • "During the third quarter of fiscal 2025, we opened 5 additional showrooms and closed 1 showroom."
  • The cash and cash equivalents balance as of November 3, 2024 was $61.7 million as compared to $37.7 million as of October 29, 2023.
  • Total merchandise inventory was $113.4 million as of November 3, 2024 as compared to $116.6 million as of October 29, 2023.

  • The Company expects the following for the full year of fiscal 2025:

    • Net sales in the range of $660 million to $680 million (prior $700 million to $735 million) vs. consensus of $712.97M.
    • Adjusted EBITDA1 in the range of $37.5 million to $48.5 million.
    • Net income in the range of $4.5 million to $12.5 million.
    • Diluted income per common share in the range of $0.27 to $0.74 on approximately 16.9 million estimated diluted weighted average shares outstanding (prior $1.01 to $1.26) vs. consensus of $2.12.
    • Fiscal 2025 will contain 52 weeks versus Fiscal 2024 which contained an additional “53rd week” in the fourth quarter.

    The Company currently expects the following for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025:

    • Net sales in the range of $221 million to $241 million vs. consensus of $268.46 million.
    • Adjusted EBITDA1 in the range of $43 million to $55 million.
    • Net income in the range of $28 million to $36 million.
    • Diluted income per common share in the range of $1.67 to $2.14 on approximately 16.8 million estimated diluted weighted average shares outstanding vs. consensus of $2.59.

data.title: Gambling.com Group acquires Odds Holdings to expand its online gambling ecosystem
data.description:
Gambling.com Group (NASDAQ:GAMB) announced on Thursday that it struck a deal to acquire Odds Holdings, which is the parent company of OddsJam.

Under the terms of the agreement, Odds Holdings stockholders will receive initial consideration of $80 million and may receive up to an additional $80 million based on Odds Holdings’ business performance through the end of 2026.

The main strategic rationale for the deal is that it expands the company’s footprint in the online gambling ecosystem with new recurring revenue from both consumer and enterprise clients.

The initial $80 million purchase consideration will be composed of $70 million in cash and $10 million in Gambling.com Group (GAMB) ordinary shares. The company expects to fund the $70 million cash payment from borrowings under its expanded credit facility.

The company said the acquisition of Odds Holdings further expands its online gambling industry footprint, adding complementary recurring revenue from new and existing users and partners.

Odds Holdings is powered by a state-of-the art technology platform for real-time odds data and offers services under multiple consumer and enterprise brands. Gambling.com Group believes the odds platform is the industry’s most advanced and delivers data to end-users with the lowest latency. The platform is built for scale, processing on average over one million requests per second and multiple terabytes of data per day, across nearly 300 sportsbooks.

The deal, which is expected to close on January 1, is anticipated to be immediately accretive to Gambling.com Group's (GAMB) operating results upon closing.

For 2024, Odds Holdings expects to generate revenue and adjusted EBITDA of approximately $26 million and $12 million, respectively. Gambling.com Group (GAMB) expects that under its management, adjusted EBITDA derived from the Odds Holdings assets will grow by at least 20% in 2025.

Shares of Gambling.com Group (GAMB) were up 1.0% in premarket trading on Thursday.

data.title: Ciena Non-GAAP EPS of $0.54 misses by $0.11, revenue of $1.12B beats by $20M
data.description:
• Ciena press release (NYSE:CIEN): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.54 misses by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $1.12B (-0.9% Y/Y) beats by $20M.
  • Cash and investments at the end of fiscal year 2024 totaled $1.33 billion.
  • Cash flow from operations totaled $349.3 million and $514.5 million for the fiscal fourth quarter and the fiscal year 2024, respectively.
  • Average days' sales outstanding were 89 and 96 for the fiscal fourth quarter and the fiscal year 2024, respectively.
  • Accounts receivable, net balance was $908.6 million.
  • Unbilled contract assets, net balance was $127.9 million.
  • Inventories totaled $820.4 million.
  • Product inventory turns were 2.7 and 2.3 for the fiscal fourth quarter and the fiscal year 2024, respectively.

data.title: Sonic Automotive acquires Audi New Orleans
data.description:
• Sonic Automotive (NYSE:SAH) said it has acquired Audi New Orleans, located in Louisiana.
  • The firm bought Audi New Orleans from the Bohn Brothers Automotive.
  • Audi New Orleans had started operations in June 2009.
  • The company's franchise automotive dealership portfolio now stands at 108 locations nationwide.

data.title: Liquidity Services beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q1 outlook
data.description:
• Liquidity Services press release (NASDAQ:LQDT): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.32 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $106.9M (+33.7% Y/Y) beats by $13.85M.
  • GMV for the fiscal fourth quarter of 2024 was $361.0 million, a 14% increase from $315.6 million in the fourth fiscal quarter of 2023.
  • Non-GAAP Adjusted EBITDA of $14.5 million, up 13%.

  • Our Q1-FY25 guidance is as follows:

    $ in millions, except per share data Q1-FY25 Guidance
    GMV $350 to $385
    GAAP Net Income $2.5 to $5.0

data.title: PHX Minerals to initiate strategic alternatives process
data.description:
• PHX Minerals (NYSE:PHX) said it will commence a process to evaluate certain strategic alternatives.
  • The alternatives could be involve a possible merger or sale of the firm.
  • The company has retained RBC Capital Markets as its financial advisor to assist in the review process.
